statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 15 of 27 countries showing critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite safe overall EU averages.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum across all fuel types (petrol 112.8 days, diesel 103.1 days, jet fuel 96.7 days), the distribution reveals concerning vulnerabilities. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, including dangerously low diesel reserves at just 30.2 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all fuel categories, highlighting significant regional disparities in fuel security.

Jet fuel emerges as the most vulnerable sector, with 15 of 27 countries below the 90-day threshold. This includes major economies like Germany (72.8 days), France (67.5 days), and Spain (42.1 days). The aviation fuel shortage spans both Eastern European nations still building strategic capacity and Western countries that may have drawn down reserves during recent Middle East supply disruptions.

Oil prices reflect ongoing market tensions, with Brent crude at $91.12/barrel representing a substantial 25.7% increase since late February escalations began. This price pressure, combined with uneven reserve distributions, suggests European fuel security remains under pressure despite adequate aggregate stockpiles. The wide price variations across member states—from Malta's €1.34/L petrol to Denmark's €2.50/L—further underscore the fragmented nature of current market conditions.
keyPoints: 15 of 27 countries show critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with jet fuel most affected
Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg face critical shortages across all three fuel types
Jet fuel reserves below 90 days in major economies including Germany (72.8 days), France (67.5 days), and Spain (42.1 days)
Brent crude up 25.7% since late February to $91.12/barrel, reflecting sustained supply concerns
Significant price disparities across EU with petrol ranging from €1.34/L in Malta to €2.50/L in Denmark
